mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly expanding equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, causing investors to downgrade earnings forecasts and sell equities over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in revolving consumer credit balances and persistent inflation indicates overheating demand that forces tighter financial conditions, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive when both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL rise concurrently, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during early economic recoveries reflects rebounding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, driving cyclical equity outperformance over short trading horizons.
failure_condition: Short-term 5-day forward returns show no correlation with INDPRO acceleration, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during recovery quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts precedes broader economic slowdown by reducing construction activity and consumer wealth, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings and negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite housing starts dropping below 1300, or the signal exhibits no directional consistency across multiple housing downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth combined with stable unemployment confirms robust labor market tightness, supporting consumer spending power and driving positive equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are flat or negative when PAYEMS and UNRATE move favorably together, or the joint signal fails to beat a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate implies expanding aggregate demand and corporate profit margins, directly supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no positive relationship with GDP growth surprises, or the signal produces negative returns during confirmed expansionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% while inflation remains elevated, real interest rates rise sharply, increasing the cost of capital and triggering risk-off positioning that depresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are high, or the interaction effect disappears in walk-forward validation over a 5-year period.
